status: internal pilot scoping draft, not buyer-ready final copy
boundary_statement: This brief describes a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review engagement. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, security assessment, deployment approval, runtime monitor, or guarantee of AI safety.
positioning: Pre-Deployment Architectural Logic Stress-Testing and Constraint Mapping Framework for Agentic Workflows
plain_english_offer: SpecShift reviews one proposed AI-agent workflow before broader deployment and maps where its reasoning, evidence handling, scope boundaries, handoffs, escalation points, and recovery paths may break under synthetic stress conditions.
ideal_pilot_target: One high-value AI-agent workflow with meaningful operational consequences.
A workflow involving multiple systems, human approvals, delegated decisions, or handoffs.
A workflow where stale data, unclear authority, weak evidence, or false completion claims would create expensive rework or risk.
A workflow still early enough that design changes are possible.
pilot_questions: What workflow are you considering or already prototyping?
What systems does the workflow touch?
Where does the agent receive inputs, and which inputs are trusted?
What actions can the agent recommend or initiate?
What happens when data is stale, incomplete, or conflicting?
What rollback, recovery, or escalation path exists if the workflow fails?
pilot_deliverables: Adversarial Stress-Test Execution Report: Findings from simulated edge-case and failure-mode scenarios.
Limitation & Boundary Map: Structured inventory of where the AI workflow's reasoning may degrade under synthetic stress conditions.
Agentic Scope & Logic Alignment Review: Architecture-stage review of scope boundaries, data freshness assumptions, system-record consistency, and handoff logic.
Escalation & Human-in-the-Loop Routing Matrix: Proposed review thresholds and escalation paths for cases where the workflow should pause, route, or require human review.
Pilot Appendix Guardrail Checklist: A practical review aid used after initial scoping to identify evidence, boundary, handoff, and recovery gaps.
not_in_scope: Real-time production monitoring or runtime intervention.
Cybersecurity penetration testing or vulnerability scanning.
IAM administration, access-control enforcement, or HR decision tooling.
Guaranteeing zero hallucination, safety, or deployment readiness.
Replacing formal human audit, legal review, HR review, or regulated professional judgment.
pilot_success_definition: A clear map of where the target workflow appears stable under synthetic stress review.
A clear map of where the workflow may require additional guardrails, review thresholds, or design changes.
A documented set of unresolved evidence, handoff, authority, data freshness, or recovery-path questions.
A practical next-step list for engineering, product, and risk teams.
pricing_posture: Priced as a professional diagnostic engagement rather than a SaaS subscription. The pilot fee covers structured stress-test execution, limitation mapping, and delivery of architecture-stage review outputs. This supports premium pilot pricing as a risk-reduction and design-review engagement without implying a software guarantee, compliance sign-off, or runtime control layer.
claim_boundary: This scoping brief stops fitting if it is used to imply validated performance, production readiness, formal compliance, runtime protection, or guaranteed safety. It should be used only to frame a bounded paid diagnostic review.
recommended_next_step: Use this brief to support a paid pilot conversation focused on one concrete workflow, then use the pilot appendix checklist only after buyer interest is established.
